THEATER PARTY.


The members of the board of the
Womans club day nursery and a
few friends will give a theater par
ty tonight at the Spokane theater.
The affair will be rendered as a
farewell tot Miss Jane Kelton, lead
ing lady Of the Lawrence stock
company, who leaves Spokane Sun
ray.


The ladies will present Miss KEEL
ton with a beautiful bouquet of
flowers. Sixteen guests will be in
cluded In the party


Z & &
MRS. REYNOLDS SPEAKS AT
MEAD.


Mrs. Minnie J. Reynolds, the
suffrage worker of New York and
Denver, will speak on the expert
ences of a Woman Voter" tomor-
row evening in the school house at
Mead, Wash. She will be assisted
by Miss Mabel Price, who will give
several humorous readings. A mu-
steal program will also be rem
=. .ered.


& & &
RETURN FROM YELLOWSTONE.
Mr.. and Mrs. d. H. Dwight and
son, Dan, returned Tuesday from
trip through Yellowstone park.
Mrs. Dwight was very much de-
lighted with her visit at this spot
of wonderland.


The Dwight family will leave
next week for their summer cot
tage at Lake Occur d'Alene.


& 4 Z


SPOKANE GUESTS.


DR.. and Mrs. Chapman of Kansas
City and their little daughter, Miss
Marette, are expected to arrive in
the city today or tomorrow and
will be guests for two weeks at
the home of DR.. and Mrs. II. l.
Parr.


WILL SPEND SUMMER AT THE


LAKES.


Mrs. ID. Fotheringham will close
her home in this city next week
